title: Gaussian density packet on M_cs: dispersion vs soliton emission
description: Initialize N as a localized Gaussian density packet with phi chosen so u = N*phi_x (still on M_cs) and ask: does the packet shed bright solitons via modulational instability (focusing NLS classic), or does the Burgers coupling alter the threshold? Analogous to BKdV T_B (Gaussian -> soliton train) but in a focusing NLS regime with Burgers boost.
ic: kappa = 1 (focusing). N(x,0) = 2.0 * exp(-(x+5)^2 / 2.25) (amplitude 2.0, width sigma=1.5). phi(x,0) = 0.3*x (constant phase gradient => phi_x = 0.3). u(x,0) = 0.3 * N(x,0). [On M_cs at t=0.]
output_shape_spec: shape (n_snapshots, 3, 256), channels (u, N, phi). Save 5+ snapshots; eval focuses on final snapshot.
phenomenon_target: Final N should contain >= 2 well-separated peaks with amplitude >= 1.0 (soliton emission, hallmark of focusing-NLS modulational instability). Mass drift < 5%. Boundedness as in T_A.
